expectations.text: Explains how to add a per-appearance Fill variant for the Dark mode in Icon Composer (e.g., selecting Dark in canvas, using Vary for Dark or equivalent per-appearance control)
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'click it and choose Vary for Dark' with detailed step-by-step including selecting Dark in canvas and using ⊕ icon next to Fill

expectations.text: Suggests a specific colour strategy for the orange in dark mode (e.g., use a brighter or more visible version of orange, or a different colour that reads on dark)
expectations.evidence: response.md table shows 'lighter/shifted orange' for dark variant

expectations.text: Explains that the primary/most recognisable element should be set to white in Mono mode
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'your primary foreground element must be white so it reads strongly over any tint colour'

expectations.text: Explains mapping secondary elements to distinct grey tones (not all the same grey) for contrast hierarchy in mono
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'For secondary elements: set them to 50–70% grey so they're visible but clearly subordinate. Make sure not everything ends up at the same grey value'

expectations.text: Mentions the Mono Options dialog (or equivalent) for previewing tint colours
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'With Mono selected, click Options (it appears near the appearance selector)'
